constraints: Node.js 18.18 or newer on Linux, macOS, and Windows.
No new mandatory runtime dependency or external service.
Canonical .sdlc evidence remains authoritative; caches and indexes remain disposable.

acceptance_criteria Effective configuration is versioned hash-locked explainable and migrated only through a reviewed plan hash; plugin upgrades cannot silently activate authority autonomy gate or budget defaults. A versioned workflow-definition and event-sourced workflow-instance engine supports software-project change-request technical-assessment and generic-governed-process presets without replacing the existing six SDLC execution phases. Requirement ceilings and a separate non-reusable delivery profile preserve an explicit autonomy choice for every pull request or local release with fail-c

# Change delivery evidence ## What was asked? Build the first trustworthy enterprise foundation for the plugin project behavior must remain stable across plugin upgrades implementation sources must be discovered correctly large canonical project state must remain queryable within measured budgets and user-facing governance must be understandable without knowing the plugin's internal vocabulary. The canonical request is REQ-ENTERPRISE-CONTROL-PLANE-001-R2 (../../../requirements/REQ-ENTERPRISE-CONTROL-PLANE-001-R2.json) delivered here through story `ST-ENT-FOUNDATION`. ## Scope and non-goals Del

approvals.summary: Refreshed only the hashes and detected package scripts for the same approved Performance evidence and boundaries; no permission or scope changed.

scope.principle: An automation approval is valid only under an explicit delegated approval level or configured automation policy recorded in the summary or evidence.
scope.approval_level: Refresh unchanged Performance references, approve the replacement work brief and exact profile for this same pull request, and reconfirm the already approved task start; no new scope, installation, external access, or release target.
